content:
First, set  
 p(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx+d,  
and define Q(t)=p(5t).  Then  
 Q(t)=125a·t^3+25b·t^2+5c·t+d,  
so Q(1)=p(5),  Q(3)=p(15),  Q(5)=p(25).  

The condition p(5)+p(25)=1906 becomes  
 Q(1)+Q(5)=1906.  
Write Q(t)=e t^3+f t^2+g t+d with e=125a, f=25b, g=5c.  Then  
 Q(1)=e+f+g+d,  
 Q(5)=125e+25f+5g+d,  
so  
 (e+125e)+(f+25f)+(g+5g)+(d+d)=1906  
⇒126e+26f+6g+2d=1906  
⇒63e+13f+3g+d=953.  

We want T=Q(3)=27e+9f+3g+d.  Eliminate d via d=953−63e−13f−3g:  
 T=27e+9f+3g+(953−63e−13f−3g)  
  =953−36e−4f.  

But e=125a and f=25b, so 36e+4f=36·125a+4·25b=4500a+100b=100(45a+b).  
Hence  
 p(15)=T=953−100k,  where k=45a+b∈ℤ.  

We seek the integer multiple of 100 nearest to 953, so that |953−100k| is as small as possible.  
Nearest multiples of 100 to 953 are 900 and 1000, giving |953−900|=53 and |953−1000|=47.  Thus the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is  
 47.  

Answer: \boxed{47}.

First, set  
 p(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx+d,  
and define Q(t)=p(5t).  Then  
 Q(t)=125a·t^3+25b·t^2+5c·t+d,  
so Q(1)=p(5),  Q(3)=p(15),  Q(5)=p(25).  

The condition p(5)+p(25)=1906 becomes  
 Q(1)+Q(5)=1906.  
Write Q(t)=e t^3+f t^2+g t+d with e=125a, f=25b, g=5c.  Then  
 Q(1)=e+f+g+d,  
 Q(5)=125e+25f+5g+d,  
so  
 (e+125e)+(f+25f)+(g+5g)+(d+d)=1906  
⇒126e+26f+6g+2d=1906  
⇒63e+13f+3g+d=953.  

We want T=Q(3)=27e+9f+3g+d.  Eliminate d via d=953−63e−13f−3g:  
 T=27e+9f+3g+(953−63e−13f−3g)  
  =953−36e−4f.  

But e=125a and f=25b, so 36e+4f=36·125a+4·25b=4500a+100b=100(45a+b).  
Hence  
 p(15)=T=953−100k,  where k=45a+b∈ℤ.  

We seek the integer multiple of 100 nearest to 953, so that |953−100k| is as small as possible.  
Nearest multiples of 100 to 953 are 900 and 1000, giving |953−900|=53 and |953−1000|=47.  Thus the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is  
 47.  

Answer: \boxed{47}.
